dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3834 IS: Expanded Telehealth Access Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Daines (for himself and Ms. Smith) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance

form.official-title: To amend title XVIII of the Social Security Act to expand the scope of practitioners eligible for payment for telehealth services under the Medicare program, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Expanded Telehealth Access Act.

section.header: Expanding the scope of practitioners eligible for payment for telehealth services under Medicare
section.text: Section 1834(m) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395m(m)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by inserting (or, in the case of an occupational therapy assistant described in paragraph (4)(E)(ii)(II) or a physical therapist assistant described in paragraph (4)(E)(ii)(III), shall pay with respect to such assistant) before located at a distant site; and

by inserting (or, in the case of an occupational therapy assistant described in paragraph (4)(E)(ii)(II) or a physical therapist assistant described in paragraph (4)(E)(ii)(III), the amount that would have been paid under this title with respect to such assistant) after would have been paid under this title;

paragraph.text: in paragraph (3)(B), by striking under such section and inserting described in subparagraph (C) of such section; and
in paragraph (4), by striking subparagraph (E) and inserting the following:

subparagraph.text: The term practitioner means any of the following:

clause.text: With respect to services furnished on or after the date of the enactment of the Expanded Telehealth Access Act—

subclause.text: a qualified audiologist (as defined in section 1861(ll)(4)(B));
a qualified occupational therapist and an occupational therapy assistant under the supervision of a qualified occupational therapist;
a qualified physical therapist and a physical therapist assistant under the supervision of a qualified physical therapist;
a qualified speech-language pathologist (as defined in section 1861(ll)(4)(A));
a facility that furnishes services described in paragraph (8) or (9) of section 1833(a) through telehealth; and
any additional provider of services or supplier, as specified by the Secretary, who is enrolled under section 1866(j) and furnishes a telehealth service.
